limitations: Autonomy protocol only; not medical advice.
Does not determine diagnosis, prognosis, treatment, trial eligibility, or expanded-access feasibility.
Does not claim that a cure or vaccine has been found.
Does not authorize use of patient-identifying data, real case data, private records, restricted datasets, credentials, or paid services.
Requires human review for clinical, legal, regulatory, sponsor, account, billing, publication, or destructive repository decisions.
Delegate outputs require validation and review before publication or reuse.
Source-backed public artifacts can still be incomplete, stale, or wrong.
The loop must stop on public-safety blockers instead of improvising around them.
A completed subphase is not a completed initiative unless the adaptive master completion audit says so.
Does not authorize residual-disease modality interpretation, outreach, expert-review claim upgrades, clinical interpretation, private-lab work, or publication.
Does not authorize public real-report quality review, laboratory-validity review, imaging interpretation, biopsy interpretation, or assay/specimen quality interpretation for a person.
Does not authorize production measurement-refusal output generation.
